user_query:
Simulate a change-aware backup of my OpenClaw configuration using a fixed backup date 2026-03-12. Read input/current_openclaw.json (a JSON object mapping file paths under ~/.openclaw to their text contents) and input/latest_backup.json (the most recent prior backup in the same format). Ignore any paths whose first segment is workspace/. Then:

1) Compute the diff vs the latest backup to determine added, modified, and deleted files (excluding workspace/).

2) If there are changes:
- Materialize a new backup under output/backup/2026-03-12/ that replicates the current files (exact text contents) excluding workspace/.
- Append a changelog entry to output/backup/changelog.md with a '## 2026-03-12' header and one bullet per changed file in relative path form, each labeled '(modified)', '(added)', or '(deleted)'.
- Write output/backup/restore-2026-03-12-preview.txt that lists all files that would be restored and includes a clear warning that restoring will overwrite the current ~/.openclaw configuration; do not actually restore anything.

3) If there are no changes:
- Do not create the date-stamped directory.
- Instead, write output/backup/no_changes.txt containing just the single line: no changes

Use only the provided input files for state; do not touch any real system paths. Write only to output/.
